title: Refactor pattern + accumulator recursion to preserve error provenance
prompt:
Given:

```
def process_all(items) do
  Enum.reduce_while(items, {:ok, []}, fn item, {:ok, acc} ->
    case transform(item) do
      {:ok, v} -> {:cont, {:ok, acc ++ [v]}}
      {:error, r} -> {:halt, {:error, {item.id, r}}}
    end
  end)
end
```

Two problems: `acc ++ [v]` is O(n²), and the reduce_while is clunkier than explicit recursion here. Rewrite as explicit multi-clause recursion with a helper `do_process/2` that accepts `{items, acc}`, prepends to acc on success, halts with `{:error, {id, reason}}` on failure, and reverses the final acc. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Batch do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Synthesis: recognize quadratic append, pick explicit recursion, preserve error provenance.
